summary: Scorching Jack Straw, tight jams and strong second‑set energy
blurb: The show kicks off with a blazing Jack Straw → Franklin's Tower that sets a high‑octane tone. Highlights include an explosive Estimated Prophet > Terrapin Station sequence, soulful Stella Blue and a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore. Overall the performance is energetic and consistently solid throughout both sets.
review: On March 31, 1987 at Philadelphia’s Spectrum the Dead delivered a night that still feels fresh in the ears of anyone who hears it. The first‑set opener—Jack Straw into Franklin's Tower—was described by multiple listeners as “scorching” and “on fire,” with Jerry’s lead guitar blazing through each transition. After a brief dip with Touch of Grey, the second set surged back to life: Estimated Prophet flowed seamlessly into Terrapin Station, giving Phil Lesh room to “bomb” with thick bass lines while Brent added color on the keyboards during Terrapin. The drum duo of Mickey and Billy kept the momentum high‑energy, especially in the mid‑set percussion burst, and Bob Weir’s vocal delivery on Desolation Row and Candyman was praised as enthusiastic and emotive. The night closed on a tender note with Brokedown Palace, leaving fans with a sense that the band was both tight and deeply feeling the music. Among the many recordings, the audience tape captured by Damico (gd1987-03-31.nak300.damico.89349.flac16) stands out for its crystal‑clear sound that feels “right by the soundboard,” preserving every nuance of this memorable performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven of nine recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the opening Jack Straw → Franklin's Tower, the Estimated Prophet > Terrapin jam and overall high energy. No consistent negative comments appear; mixed reviews only note minor audio quirks, so a robust positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience capture that sounds like a soundboard feed—crisp, full‑bodied, no dropouts—and includes all the show’s standout moments.

key_highlights: Scorching Jack Straw → Franklin's Tower opening sets an explosive tone.
Estimated Prophet > Terrapin Station jam delivers powerful improvisation.
Second set rebounds with high‑energy drums, Stella Blue and a moving Brokedown Palace encore.
Damico audience recording praised for superb sound quality that feels “right by the soundboard.”

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching solos throughout, especially on Jack Straw, Franklin's Tower, Candyman and Stella; tone described as crisp and fiery.
band_performance.Phil: Bombs with strong bass lines, adds chime work on Ramble On Rose and provides solid support across the set.
band_performance.Bob: Roars vocally, delivering enthusiastic performances on Desolation Row, Candyman and Brokedown Palace.
band_performance.Brent: Provides subtle background color on Desolation Row and shines with inspired keyboard work during Terrapin Station.
band_performance.Mickey: Lively, high‑energy drumming; drums described as pounding in the first set and driving the second‑set momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Solid, driving beats that complement Mickey’s energy; noted for maintaining a powerful rhythmic foundation throughout.
